item.title: Defense Group KNDS Plans to Cut its Stake in Gearbox Maker Renk
item.description: KNDS NV is looking to sell part of its stake in Renk Group AG as the Franco-German tankmaker prepares up for its own initial public offering.

item.title: Trump Team Seeks Longer Gas Tax Holiday With Phased-In Return
item.description: The Trump administration is working with Congress to suspend federal excise taxes on gasoline and diesel as the war in Iran drives up oil and fuel costs.

item.title: Chile Lowers Copper Production Outlook, Raises Price Forecasts
item.description: Top copper supplier Chile lowered its production forecasts for this year and next, reinforcing expectations of tight global supplies that have pushed prices near record highs.

item.title: US Open Stadium Seeks $400 Million Private Debt for Revamp Plans
item.description: The United States Tennis Association is in talks with institutional investors to borrow at least $400 million of private credit to refurbish New York’s Arthur Ashe Stadium.

item.title: Warsh Edges Closer to Divestment Promise Ahead of Swearing In
item.description: Kevin Warsh has sold most of the financial holdings he promised to divest from before becoming chair of the Federal Reserve, a key step in addressing a point of controversy surrounding his confirmation, a new government record showed.

item.title: Google to Unveil New AI and Search Features at Developers Conference
item.description: Members of the media view Google products during the Google I/O event in Mountain View, California. Photographer: Josh Edelson/AFP/Getty Images

item.title: Williams CEO Sees Gas Demand Outpacing Past Years on Powering AI
item.description: Demand for US natural gas is set to rise in the next 10 years more than it did for the past 15 years amid growing power needs from data centers supporting artificial intelligence and a global push for energy security, according to the head of Williams Cos.

item.title: Sony Launches $650 Headphones Made With Steel and Vegan Leather
item.description: Sony Group Corp. introduced a $650 pair of high-end wireless headphones, setting a new ceiling for the company in a segment that has already grown more pricey in recent years.

item.title: Euronext Results Beat on Volatility-Led Trading, Greek Exchange
item.description: Euronext NV’s results beat expectations as market volatility boosted trading volumes and the newly acquired Athens stock exchange contributed to revenue.

item.title: Apple’s New Hardware Chief Shakes Up Oversight of Product Design
item.description: Apple Inc. Chief Hardware Officer Johny Srouji is reorganizing hardware development and shifting oversight of key functions such as product design, part of an effort to speed up work on future devices.

item.title: BPCL Turns to Spot Crude as Iran Conflict Disrupts Term Supplies
item.description: India’s Bharat Petroleum Corp. Ltd. has largely shifted to making spot crude purchases as the Iran war upends annual arrangements for Middle Eastern barrels, highlighting challenges for refiners to secure supplies.

item.title: OpenAI Founding Member Andrej Karpathy Takes Role at Anthropic
item.description: Andrej Karpathy, a founding member of OpenAI who later went to work for Tesla Inc., has joined Anthropic PBC to do research and development focused on helping train new artificial intelligence models.

item.title: Fed’s Perli Says T-Bill Purchases Can Be Adjusted Up or Down
item.description: Federal Reserve Bank of New York’s Roberto Perli said the central bank’s monthly Treasury bill purchases are not on a pre-determined course and can be adjusted depending on market conditions.

item.title: KKR and ECP Weigh Increasing Offer for Energy Group DCC
item.description: KKR & Co. and Energy Capital Partners are considering raising their offer to acquire energy provider DCC Plc, people with knowledge of the matter said.

item.title: Ometto Weighs Buying Cosan Farm Assets Instead of Funding Raízen
item.description: Billionaire Rubens Ometto is considering acquiring part of the farmland portfolio held by Radar, Cosan SA’s land management unit, according to people familiar with the matter, a move that could derail the entrepreneur’s plans to inject fresh capital into Raízen SA.

item.title: Cisco Shares Dip After Company Discusses Margin ‘Headwind’
item.description: Cisco Systems Inc. shares slipped after Chief Financial Officer Mark Patterson warned that the company would see “ups and downs” with its gross profit margin as it pushes further into AI infrastructure.

item.title: Tata’s UK Plant Wins Exemption From US Steel Tariff Rules
item.description: Tata Steel UK has secured a US tariff exemption that will allow it to export steel processed in Britain under the lower rate applied to UK shipments, even though the raw material was made overseas.

item.title: Irish Officials Told to Probe ‘Suspicious’ Polymarket Betting
item.description: Ireland’s Finance Minister Simon Harris has instructed officials to carry out an analysis of how the government should react to the uptick of “suspicious betting” on platforms like Polymarket.

item.title: German Gas Storage to Fill Up Without Aid, Market Manager Says
item.description: Germany’s gas market manager reiterated that it will not intervene on behalf of the government to refill the country’s vast storage facilities, saying that levels should start rising soon.

item.title: Niger Boosts Stake in China-Backed Pipeline as Beijing Seeks to Secure Supply
item.description: Niger secured a stake in a China-backed oil pipeline after renegotiating export terms, as Beijing steps up efforts to secure reliable crude supplies amid global market disruptions.

item.title: College Kids Don’t Want Your AI
item.description: The unique threat artificial intelligence poses to students’ education and their hopes of someday landing a job has made the university a powerful crucible for anti-AI resistance.

item.title: La Caisse Arm Buys Database Firm ISC in $872 Million Deal
item.description: The infrastructure arm of Caisse de dépôt et placement du Québec will take Canadian database company Information Services Corp. private in a deal worth C$1.2 billion ($872 million).

item.title: Polish Lawmakers Want Central Bank Governor to Testify in Probe
item.description: Polish central bank Governor Adam Glapinski will be called to testify before a parliamentary committee following renewed conflict with the ruling coalition.

item.title: Shell Wins Investor Backing for Higher Executive Bonuses
item.description: Shell Plc shareholders approved a new policy that could award larger bonuses to executive directors, moving the company closer to compensation levels at its US rivals.

item.title: Distressed-Debt Deals Often End in ‘Hard Default,’ Moody’s Warns
item.description: About a quarter of companies that go through a distressed exchange event end up in so-called “hard defaults” such as bankruptcy or a missed payment, according to a Moody’s Analytics report.

item.title: US Pending Home Sales Increase for a Third Straight Month
item.description: Pending sales of previously owned US homes rose for a third straight month in April, indicating firmer underlying demand at the start of the spring selling season.

item.title: Bessent Tells Allies ‘No Room for Excuses’ on Iran Sanctions
item.description: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent exhorted US allies to join the US in “aggressively” enforcing sanctions on Iran and other malicious actors and said there was “no room for excuses” for those who wish to crack down on terror financing.

item.title: High Speed 2 to Cost up to £103 Billion After More Delays
item.description: The UK’s High Speed 2 rail line will cost between £87.7 billion ($117 billion) and £102.7 billion and open by 2036 at the earliest, Transport Secretary Heidi Alexander said, revealing the latest budget increase and delay to the troubled project.

item.title: French Grid Keeps Nuclear Reactors Online Despite Solar Surge
item.description: France’s grid operator is keeping extra nuclear reactors online to keep the system stable despite strong solar power generation, contributing to a supply surplus and deeply discounted electricity prices.

item.title: US 30-Year Yield Hits Highest Since 2007 on Inflation Angst
item.description: Yields on the US Treasury’s longest-dated bond rose to the highest level in almost two decades as investor concern over accelerating inflation fuels a selloff across global debt markets.
